","type":"text/x-markdown"},"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/README.md":{"title":"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/README.md","text":"# JSON DSL

> Declarative JSON builder widgets for TiddlyWiki

Compose JSON structures using widget syntax instead of string manipulation.

## Key features

* **Composable widget set** -- `<$json-obj>`, `<$json-array>`, `<$json-prop>`, `<$json-val>`, `<$json-item>`
* **Output widgets** -- emit to tiddler field (`<$json-save>`) or action variable (`<$json-emit>`)
* **Debug widget** -- `<$json-debug>` for inspecting output during development
* **Widget tree integration** -- seamless integration with TiddlyWiki's widget tree and filter expressions

## Prerequisites

No external prerequisites.

## Quick start

Build JSON declaratively: `<$json-obj><$json-prop name="key"><$json-val value="hello"/></$json-prop></$json-obj>`. Use `<$json-emit>` to output the result.

## Plugin Library

Install from the [rimir plugin library](https://rimir-cc.github.io/tw-plugin-library/) via *Control Panel → Plugins → Get more plugins*.

## Demo

Try this plugin in the [live demo wiki](https://rimir-cc.github.io/tw-demo/).

## License

MIT -- see [LICENSE.md](LICENSE.md)

"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/doc/architecture":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/doc/architecture","created":"20260305110625328","modified":"20260305110625328","rdt.order":"2","rdt.plugin-key":"json-dsl","rdt.plugin-name":"json dsl","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Declarative JSON builder widgets for TiddlyWiki","rdt.section":"Architecture","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Builder context

All widgets share a `jsonBuilderContext` object that propagates down the widget tree via `parentWidget` traversal. The context contains:

|!Field |!Type |!Purpose |
|`root` |any |The top-level JSON value being built |
|`stack` |array |Stack of "current containers" — the innermost array or object receives new values |
|`captureStack` |array |Used by `<$json-val>` for child-capture semantics |
|`debug` |boolean |When `true`, warnings are collected |
|`warnings` |array |Accumulated warning messages for `<$json-debug>` |

!! Widget roles

|!Widget |!Creates |!Attaches to parent via |
|`<$json-obj>` |`{}` |`key` attr (on object parent), push (on array parent), or becomes root |
|`<$json-array>` |`[]` |Same attachment logic as `<$json-obj>` |
|`<$json-prop>` |key/value pair |Sets property on nearest object on stack |
|`<$json-val>` |scalar or child-captured value |Pushes to nearest array on stack, or capture-slot |
|`<$json-item>` |coerced scalar |Pushes to nearest array on stack |
|`<$json-emit>` |text node |Reads `ctx.root`, calls `JSON.stringify` |
|`<$json-save>` |— (side-effect) |Reads `ctx.root`, calls `wiki.setText()` |
|`<$json-debug>` |text node |Dumps warnings and raw tree |

!! Execution flow

# `<$json-obj>` or `<$json-array>` at the top level creates the context and sets `ctx.root`
# Nested widgets call `getJsonContext(this)` to walk up the parent chain and find the context
# Container widgets (`<$json-obj>`, `<$json-array>`) push themselves onto `ctx.stack` during `execute()` and pop in `render()`
# Leaf widgets (`<$json-prop>`, `<$json-item>`) peek at the top of `ctx.stack` and write into it
# Terminal widgets (`<$json-emit>`, `<$json-save>`) read `ctx.root` after the tree is fully built

!! Scalar array mode

When `<$json-array>` has both `filter` and `type` attributes, it enters scalar mode:

* Each filter result is coerced via `coerceValue()` and pushed directly to the array
* No child widgets are iterated — this is a performance shortcut for simple lists
* Uses the same coercion logic as `<$json-item>`

!! Design decisions

* ''Stack-based context'' — avoids passing data through widget attributes; any depth of nesting works naturally
* ''Capture semantics'' — `<$json-val>` uses a capture slot so its child can be an object or array, not just a scalar
* ''Refresh = refreshSelf'' — JSON DSL trees are rebuilt entirely on any change, keeping the logic stateless
* ''No DOM output from structural widgets'' — only `<$json-emit>` and `<$json-debug>` create DOM nodes

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="warning">
<$fill $name="body">
The entire JSON tree is rebuilt on every refresh cycle. For very large structures, consider caching the output via `<$json-save>` into a data tiddler.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/doc/examples":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/doc/examples","created":"20260305110625328","modified":"20260305110625328","rdt.order":"3","rdt.plugin-key":"json-dsl","rdt.plugin-name":"json dsl","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Declarative JSON builder widgets for TiddlyWiki","rdt.section":"Examples","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Simple object

```
<$json-obj>
  <$json-prop key="title" value="Hello World"/>
  <$json-prop key="enabled" value="true" type="boolean"/>
  <$json-emit/>
</$json-obj>
```

Output: `{"title": "Hello World", "enabled": true}`

!! Nested objects

```
<$json-obj>
  <$json-prop key="name" value="Alice"/>
  <$json-obj key="address">
    <$json-prop key="city" value="Berlin"/>
    <$json-prop key="zip" value="10115"/>
  </$json-obj>
  <$json-emit/>
</$json-obj>
```

!! Array of objects from filter

```
<$json-obj>
  <$json-array key="projects" filter="[tag[project]]">
    <$json-obj>
      <$json-prop key="title" filter="[<item>get[title]]"/>
      <$json-prop key="status" filter="[<item>get[status]]"/>
    </$json-obj>
  </$json-array>
  <$json-emit/>
</$json-obj>
```

!! Scalar array (shorthand)

When you just need an array of strings or numbers from a filter, use the `type` attribute directly on `<$json-array>`:

```
<$json-obj>
  <$json-array key="tags" filter="[tag[project]]" type="string"/>
  <$json-array key="counts" filter="[tag[project]get[count]]" type="number"/>
  <$json-emit/>
</$json-obj>
```

!! Save to tiddler field

Use `<$json-save>` to write the built JSON into a tiddler field instead of rendering it as text:

```
<$json-obj>
  <$json-prop key="name" value="Config"/>
  <$json-prop key="version" value="1" type="number"/>
  <$json-save $tiddler="MyConfig" $field="text" indent="2"/>
</$json-obj>
```

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="tip">
<$fill $name="body">
`<$json-save>` produces no visible output. Combine it with `<$json-emit>` if you want to both save and display the JSON.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

!! Debug mode

Wrap your tree in `debug="yes"` and add `<$json-debug>` to see warnings:

```
<$json-obj debug="yes">
  <$json-prop key="x" value="1"/>
  <$json-prop key="x" value="2"/>
  <$json-debug/>
</$json-obj>
```

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/doc/overview":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/doc/overview","created":"20260305110625329","modified":"20260305110625329","rdt.hub-category":"infrastructure","rdt.hub-category-name":"Infrastructure","rdt.hub-order":"2","rdt.order":"1","rdt.plugin-key":"json-dsl","rdt.plugin-name":"json dsl","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Declarative JSON builder widgets for TiddlyWiki","rdt.section":"Overview","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! What it does

JSON DSL provides a set of widgets that let you build JSON data structures declaratively in TiddlyWiki markup. Instead of hand-writing JSON strings, you compose `<$json-obj>`, `<$json-array>`, `<$json-prop>`, and other widgets — they assemble the tree at render time.

!! Features

* ''Declarative composition'' — nest `<$json-obj>`, `<$json-array>`, `<$json-prop>`, `<$json-val>`, and `<$json-item>` to build any JSON structure
* ''Filter integration'' — `<$json-prop>` and `<$json-array>` accept TiddlyWiki `filter` attributes to pull values from the wiki store
* ''Type coercion'' — `type` attribute on `<$json-prop>`, `<$json-val>`, and `<$json-item>` for `string`, `number`, `boolean`, `null`, `raw`
* ''Scalar arrays'' — `<$json-array filter="..." type="string"/>` produces arrays of coerced scalars directly, no child widgets needed
* ''Text output'' — `<$json-emit>` renders the assembled JSON as a text node (with configurable indent)
* ''Field output'' — `<$json-save>` writes the assembled JSON into a tiddler field
* ''Debug mode'' — `<$json-obj debug="yes">` enables `<$json-debug>` to display warnings and the raw tree

!! Quick start

Build a JSON object from tiddler fields:

```
<$json-obj>
  <$json-prop key="name" value="My Project"/>
  <$json-prop key="count" value="42" type="number"/>
  <$json-array key="tags" filter="[tag[project]]" type="string"/>
  <$json-emit indent="2"/>
</$json-obj>
```

Output:

```json
{
  "name": "My Project",
  "count": 42,
  "tags": ["ProjectA", "ProjectB"]
}
```

<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/callout" type="info">
<$fill $name="body">
JSON DSL widgets are browser-side only. They build the JSON tree during render and output it as text or write it to a tiddler field.
</$fill>
</$transclude>

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/doc/reference":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/doc/reference","created":"20260305110625329","modified":"20260305110625329","rdt.order":"4","rdt.plugin-key":"json-dsl","rdt.plugin-name":"json dsl","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Declarative JSON builder widgets for TiddlyWiki","rdt.section":"Reference","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! `<$json-obj>`

Creates a JSON object `{}` and pushes it onto the builder stack.

|!Attribute |!Default |!Description |
|`key` |— |Property name when nested inside another object |
|`debug` |`"no"` |Set to `"yes"` to enable warning collection |

!! `<$json-array>`

Creates a JSON array `[]` and pushes it onto the builder stack.

|!Attribute |!Default |!Description |
|`key` |— |Property name when nested inside an object |
|`filter` |— |TiddlyWiki filter; results become iteration items |
|`type` |— |When set with `filter`: coerce results to scalar type (`string`, `number`, `boolean`, `null`, `raw`) and skip child iteration |
|`variable` |`"item"` |Variable name for current item during iteration |

!! `<$json-prop>`

Sets a key/value pair on the nearest object on the stack.

|!Attribute |!Default |!Description |
|`key` |— |Property name (required) |
|`value` |`""` |Literal value |
|`filter` |— |TiddlyWiki filter; first result becomes the value |
|`type` |`"string"` |Type coercion: `string`, `number`, `boolean`/`bool`, `null`, `raw` |
|`omit-empty` |`"no"` |When `"yes"`, skip the property if value is empty string |

!! `<$json-val>`

Pushes a value onto the nearest array. Supports child-capture: if a child `<$json-obj>` or `<$json-array>` is nested inside, it captures that as the value.

|!Attribute |!Default |!Description |
|`value` |`""` |Literal value |
|`type` |`"string"` |Type coercion |

!! `<$json-item>`

Pushes a coerced scalar onto the nearest array. Simpler than `<$json-val>` — no child capture.

|!Attribute |!Default |!Description |
|`value` |`""` |Literal value |
|`type` |`"string"` |Type coercion |

!! `<$json-emit>`

Renders the built JSON tree as a text node.

|!Attribute |!Default |!Description |
|`indent` |`2` |Indentation spaces for `JSON.stringify` |

!! `<$json-save>`

Writes the built JSON tree into a tiddler field. Produces no DOM output.

|!Attribute |!Default |!Description |
|`$tiddler` |— |Target tiddler title (required) |
|`$field` |`"text"` |Target field name |
|`indent` |`2` |Indentation spaces for `JSON.stringify` |

!! `<$json-debug>`

Displays warnings and the raw JSON tree. Only useful when `debug="yes"` is set on the root `<$json-obj>`.

!! Type coercion table

|!Type value |!Behavior |
|`string` |`String(value)` |
|`number` |`Number(value)`, empty string becomes `null` |
|`boolean` / `bool` |`true` for `"true"`, `"yes"`, `"1"` (case-insensitive); `false` otherwise |
|`null` |Always `null` |
|`raw` |`JSON.parse(value)`, falls back to raw string on parse error |

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/documentation":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/documentation","created":"20260305110625329","modified":"20260305110625329","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<%if [[$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template]has[plugin-type]] %>
<$tiddler tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/doc/overview">

</$tiddler>
<%else%>

See the [[readme|$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/readme]] tab for a basic overview.
<%endif%>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/examples/001/data/MyProjectGroup/P1":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/examples/001/data/MyProjectGroup/P1","created":"20260221100212401","modified":"20260305110625330","parent":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/examples/001/data/MyProjectGroup","status":"planning","tags":"","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/examples/001/data/MyProjectGroup/P2":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/examples/001/data/MyProjectGroup/P2","created":"20260221102403633","modified":"20260305110625330","parent":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/examples/001/data/MyProjectGroup","status":"planning","tags":"","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/examples/001/data/MyProjectGroup":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/examples/001/data/MyProjectGroup","created":"20260221100045483","modified":"20260305110625330","name":"My-Project","short-description":"my desc blabla..","tags":"","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/examples/001":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/examples/001","created":"20260221095908195","modified":"20260305110625329","tags":"","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"\whitespace trim
<$let pg="$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/examples/001/data/MyProjectGroup">

<$json-obj debug="yes">

  <!-- 1) simple attribute-mode properties -->
  <$json-prop key="name" filter="[<pg>get[title]]"/>
  <$json-prop key="description" filter="[<pg>get[description]]" omit-empty="yes"/>
  <$json-prop key="active" value="yes" type="boolean"/>
  <$json-prop key="version" value={{{[[3]add[4]]}}} type="number"/>

  <!-- 2) scalar nested body value via json-val -->
  <$json-prop key="meta">
    <$json-obj>
      <$json-prop key="generatedBy" value="json-dsl test"/>
      <$json-prop key="countHint">
        <$json-val value="42" type="number"/>
      </$json-prop>
      <$json-prop key="isDemo">
        <$json-val value="true" type="boolean"/>
      </$json-prop>
      <$json-prop key="nullableExample">
        <$json-val type="null"/>
      </$json-prop>
    </$json-obj>
  </$json-prop>

  <!-- 3) array with direct scalar items -->
  <$json-array key="staticTags">
    <$json-item value="alpha"/>
    <$json-item value="beta"/>
    <$json-item value="3" type="number"/>
    <$json-item value="true" type="boolean"/>
    <$json-item type="null"/>
    <$json-item value='{"demo":"raw-object"}' type="raw"/>
  </$json-array>

  <!-- 4) array captured as property body, using core $list -->
  <$json-prop key="projects">
    <$json-array>

      <$list filter="[parent<pg>]" variable="project">

        <$json-obj>
          <!-- scalar props from project tiddler -->
          <$json-prop key="title" filter="[<project>get[title]]"/>
          <$json-prop key="id" filter="[<project>get[id]]" omit-empty="yes"/>

          <!-- conditional property using TW conditional shortcut -->
          <%if [<project>has[status]] %>
            <$json-prop key="status" filter="[<project>get[status]]"/>
          <%endif%>

          <!-- number/bool coercion -->
          <$json-prop key="priority" filter="[<project>get[priority]]" type="number" omit-empty="yes"/>
          <$json-prop key="archived" filter="[<project>get[archived]]" type="boolean" omit-empty="yes"/>

          <!-- nested object in property body -->
          <$json-prop key="owner">
            <$json-obj>
              <$json-prop key="name" filter="[<project>get[owner]]" omit-empty="yes"/>
              <$json-prop key="email" filter="[<project>get[owner-email]]" omit-empty="yes"/>
            </$json-obj>
          </$json-prop>

          <!-- nested scalar body -->
          <$json-prop key="budget">
            <$json-val value={{{ [<project>get[budget]] }}} type="number"/>
          </$json-prop>

          <!-- nested array attached by key (json-array key=...) -->
          <$json-array key="labels">
            <!-- project title as one label -->
            <$json-item value={{{ [<project>get[title]] }}}/>
            <!-- static labels -->
            <$json-item value="project"/>
            <!-- raw JSON scalar/object -->
            <$json-item value='{"kind":"label-meta"}' type="raw"/>
          </$json-array>

          <!-- nested array in property-body mode, with filter-driven loop -->
          <$json-prop key="tasks">
            <$json-array filter="[parent<project>]" variable="task">

              <$json-obj>
                <$json-prop key="title" filter="[<task>get[title]]"/>

                <%if [<task>has[status]] %>
                  <$json-prop key="status" filter="[<task>get[status]]"/>
                <%endif%>

                <$json-prop key="estimate" filter="[<task>get[estimate]]" type="number" omit-empty="yes"/>
                <$json-prop key="done" filter="[<task>get[done]]" type="boolean" omit-empty="yes"/>

                <!-- nested object inside task -->
                <$json-prop key="notes">
                  <$json-obj>
                    <$json-prop key="summary" filter="[<task>get[summary]]" omit-empty="yes"/>
                    <$json-prop key="details" filter="[<task>get[text]]" omit-empty="yes"/>
                  </$json-obj>
                </$json-prop>

                <!-- nested scalar capture -->
                <$json-prop key="rank">
                  <$json-val value={{{ [<task>get[rank]] }}} type="number"/>
                </$json-prop>

                <!-- array of scalar items -->
                <$json-array key="flags">
                  <$json-item value="task"/>
                  <%if [<task>has[status]] %>
                    <$json-item value={{{ [<task>get[status]] }}}/>
                  <%endif%>
                </$json-array>

              </$json-obj>

            </$json-array>
          </$json-prop>

          <!-- example of raw property value -->
          <$json-prop key="rawPayload" value='{"source":"demo","ok":true}' type="raw"/>

        </$json-obj>

      </$list>

    </$json-array>
  </$json-prop>

  <!-- 5) final output -->
  <$json-emit indent="2"/>

  <!-- 6) debug (must be INSIDE json tree) -->
  <!--- DEBUG (warnings) --->
  <$json-debug mode="warnings"/>

  <!----- DEBUG (full) --->
  <$json-debug mode="full"/>

</$json-obj>
</$let>


"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/icon":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/icon","created":"20260305110625330","modified":"20260305110625330","type":"image/svg+xml","text":"<svg width="22pt" height="22pt" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="maroon" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
<path d="M8 3H7a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v4a2 2 0 0 1-2 2 2 2 0 0 1 2 2v4a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h1"/>
<path d="M16 3h1a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v4a2 2 0 0 0 2 2 2 2 0 0 0-2 2v4a2 2 0 0 1-2 2h-1"/>
</svg>"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-array.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-array.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/yourname/json-dsl/json-array.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: widget
\*/
(function(){

/*jslint node: true, browser: true */
/*global $tw: false */
"use strict";

var Widget = require("$:/core/modules/widgets/widget.js").widget;

function coerceValue(value,type) {
  switch(type) {
    case "string": return String(value);
    case "number": return (value === "" ? null : Number(value));
    case "boolean":
    case "bool": return /^(true|yes|1)$/i.test(String(value));
    case "null": return null;
    case "raw": try { return JSON.parse(value); } catch(e) { return value; }
    default: return String(value);
  }
}

function getJsonContext(widget) {
  var w = widget;
  while(w) {
    if(w.jsonBuilderContext) {
      return w.jsonBuilderContext;
    }
    w = w.parentWidget;
  }
  return null;
}

function JsonArrayWidget(parseTreeNode,options) {
  this.initialise(parseTreeNode,options);
}
JsonArrayWidget.prototype = new Widget();

JsonArrayWidget.prototype.render = function(parent,nextSibling) {
  this.parentDomNode = parent;
  this.execute();

  // Scalar mode: filter + type → push coerced values directly, no child iteration
  if(this.scalarType && this.items) {
    var arr = this.jsonBuilderContext.stack[this.jsonBuilderContext.stack.length - 1];
    for(var i = 0; i < this.items.length; i++) {
      arr.push(coerceValue(this.items[i], this.scalarType));
    }
  } else if(!this.items) {
    this.renderChildren(parent,nextSibling);
  } else {
    for(var i = 0; i < this.items.length; i++) {
      var item = this.items[i];
      this.makeChildWidgets();

      if(this.iterationVariable) {
        this.setVariable(this.iterationVariable,item);
      }
      this.setVariable("currentTiddler",item);
      this.setVariable("item",item);

      this.renderChildren(parent,nextSibling);
    }
  }

  if(this.jsonBuilderContext && this.didPushToStack) {
    this.jsonBuilderContext.stack.pop();
  }
};

JsonArrayWidget.prototype.execute = function() {
  this.computeAttributes();
  this.key = this.getAttribute("key",null);
  this.filter = this.getAttribute("filter",null);
  this.scalarType = this.getAttribute("type",null);
  this.iterationVariable = this.getAttribute("variable","item");

  this.makeChildWidgets();

  var ctx = getJsonContext(this);
  if(!ctx || typeof ctx !== "object") {
    ctx = {root: null, stack: [], captureStack: [], debug: false, warnings: []};
  } else {
    ctx.captureStack = ctx.captureStack || [];
    ctx.warnings = ctx.warnings || [];
  }

  var arr = [];
  var parentNode = ctx.stack.length ? ctx.stack[ctx.stack.length - 1] : null;
  var capture = ctx.captureStack.length ? ctx.captureStack[ctx.captureStack.length - 1] : null;

  // Capture only if not already claimed
  if(capture && !capture.claimed) {
    if(!capture.hasValue) {
      capture.value = arr;
      capture.hasValue = true;
      capture.claimed = true; // <-- consume capture for direct child only
    } else if(ctx.debug) {
      ctx.warnings.push("json-array: capture collision (extra nested value ignored)");
    }
  } else if(parentNode && !Array.isArray(parentNode)) {
    if(this.key) {
      parentNode[this.key] = arr;
    } else if(ctx.root === null) {
      ctx.root = arr;
    } else if(ctx.debug) {
      ctx.warnings.push("json-array: unattached array (missing key on object parent)");
    }
  } else if(Array.isArray(parentNode)) {
    parentNode.push(arr);
  } else if(ctx.root === null) {
    ctx.root = arr;
  } else if(ctx.debug) {
    ctx.warnings.push("json-array: unattached array (root already set)");
  }

  ctx.stack.push(arr);
  this.didPushToStack = true;
  this.jsonBuilderContext = ctx;

  this.items = this.filter ? this.wiki.filterTiddlers(this.filter,this) : null;
};

JsonArrayWidget.prototype.refresh = function(changedTiddlers) {
  this.refreshSelf();
  return true;
};

exports["json-array"] = JsonArrayWidget;

})();
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"widget","created":"20260221095744552","modified":"20260305110625330","tags":""},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-debug.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-debug.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/yourname/json-dsl/json-debug.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: widget
\*/
(function(){
"use strict";
var Widget = require("$:/core/modules/widgets/widget.js").widget;

function getJsonContext(widget){ var w=widget; while(w){ if(w.jsonBuilderContext){return w.jsonBuilderContext;} w=w.parentWidget; } return null; }

function JsonDebugWidget(parseTreeNode,options){ this.initialise(parseTreeNode,options); }
JsonDebugWidget.prototype = new Widget();

JsonDebugWidget.prototype.render = function(parent,nextSibling){
  this.parentDomNode = parent;
  this.execute();
  var pre = this.document.createElement("pre");
  pre.appendChild(this.document.createTextNode(this.outputText || ""));
  parent.insertBefore(pre,nextSibling);
  this.domNodes.push(pre);
};

JsonDebugWidget.prototype.execute = function() {
  this.computeAttributes();
  var mode = this.getAttribute("mode","full");
  var ctx = getJsonContext(this);

  if(!ctx) {
    this.outputText = "json-debug: no json-builder-context (place inside <$json-obj>/<$json-array>)";
    return;
  }

  var payload = (mode === "warnings") ? {
    debug: !!ctx.debug,
    warnings: ctx.warnings || []
  } : (mode === "root" ? ctx.root : {
    debug: !!ctx.debug,
    root: ctx.root,
    stackDepth: (ctx.stack || []).length,
    captureDepth: (ctx.captureStack || []).length,
    warnings: ctx.warnings || []
  });

  this.outputText = JSON.stringify(payload,null,2);
};

JsonDebugWidget.prototype.refresh = function(changedTiddlers){ this.refreshSelf(); return true; };

exports["json-debug"] = JsonDebugWidget;
})();
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"widget","created":"20260221101130633","modified":"20260305110625330","tags":""},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-emit.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-emit.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/yourname/json-dsl/json-emit.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: widget
\*/
(function(){
"use strict";
var Widget = require("$:/core/modules/widgets/widget.js").widget;

function getJsonContext(widget){ var w=widget; while(w){ if(w.jsonBuilderContext){return w.jsonBuilderContext;} w=w.parentWidget; } return null; }

function JsonEmitWidget(parseTreeNode,options){ this.initialise(parseTreeNode,options); }
JsonEmitWidget.prototype = new Widget();

JsonEmitWidget.prototype.render = function(parent,nextSibling){
  this.parentDomNode = parent;
  this.execute();
  var textNode = this.document.createTextNode(this.outputText || "");
  parent.insertBefore(textNode,nextSibling);
  this.domNodes.push(textNode);
};

JsonEmitWidget.prototype.execute = function() {
  this.computeAttributes();
  var indent = parseInt(this.getAttribute("indent","2"),10);
  if(isNaN(indent)) indent = 2;

  var ctx = getJsonContext(this);
  try {
    this.outputText = JSON.stringify(ctx ? ctx.root : null, null, indent);
  } catch (e) {
    this.outputText = "{\"error\":\"JSON emit failed\"}";
  }
};

JsonEmitWidget.prototype.refresh = function(changedTiddlers){ this.refreshSelf(); return true; };

exports["json-emit"] = JsonEmitWidget;
})();
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"widget","created":"20260221095844850","modified":"20260305110625330","tags":""},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-item.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-item.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/yourname/json-dsl/json-item.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: widget
\*/
(function(){
"use strict";
var Widget = require("$:/core/modules/widgets/widget.js").widget;

function getJsonContext(widget){ var w=widget; while(w){ if(w.jsonBuilderContext){return w.jsonBuilderContext;} w=w.parentWidget; } return null; }

function JsonItemWidget(parseTreeNode,options){ this.initialise(parseTreeNode,options); }
JsonItemWidget.prototype = new Widget();

JsonItemWidget.prototype.render = function(parent,nextSibling){ this.parentDomNode = parent; this.execute(); };

JsonItemWidget.prototype.execute = function() {
  this.computeAttributes();
  var ctx = getJsonContext(this), current, value, type;
  if(!ctx || !ctx.stack || !ctx.stack.length) return;
  current = ctx.stack[ctx.stack.length-1];
  if(!Array.isArray(current)) return;

  value = this.getAttribute("value","");
  type = (this.getAttribute("type","string") || "string").toLowerCase();
  current.push(this.coerceValue(value,type));
};

JsonItemWidget.prototype.coerceValue = function(value,type){
  switch(type){
    case "string": return String(value);
    case "number": return (value === "" ? null : Number(value));
    case "boolean":
    case "bool": return /^(true|yes|1)$/i.test(String(value));
    case "null": return null;
    case "raw": try { return JSON.parse(value); } catch(e) { return value; }
    default: return String(value);
  }
};

JsonItemWidget.prototype.refresh = function(changedTiddlers){ this.refreshSelf(); return true; };

exports["json-item"] = JsonItemWidget;
})();
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"widget","created":"20260221095807588","modified":"20260305110625330","tags":""},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-obj.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-obj.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/yourname/json-dsl/json-obj.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: widget
\*/
(function(){

/*jslint node: true, browser: true */
/*global $tw: false */
"use strict";

var Widget = require("$:/core/modules/widgets/widget.js").widget;

function getJsonContext(widget) {
  var w = widget;
  while(w) {
    if(w.jsonBuilderContext) {
      return w.jsonBuilderContext;
    }
    w = w.parentWidget;
  }
  return null;
}

function JsonObjWidget(parseTreeNode,options) {
  this.initialise(parseTreeNode,options);
}
JsonObjWidget.prototype = new Widget();

JsonObjWidget.prototype.render = function(parent,nextSibling) {
  this.parentDomNode = parent;
  this.execute();
  this.renderChildren(parent,nextSibling);

  if(this.jsonBuilderContext && this.didPushToStack) {
    this.jsonBuilderContext.stack.pop();
  }
};

JsonObjWidget.prototype.execute = function() {
  this.computeAttributes();
  this.makeChildWidgets();

  var ctx = getJsonContext(this);
  if(!ctx || typeof ctx !== "object") {
    ctx = {
      root: null,
      stack: [],
      captureStack: [],
      debug: false,
      warnings: []
    };
  } else {
    ctx.captureStack = ctx.captureStack || [];
    ctx.warnings = ctx.warnings || [];
  }

  if(this.getAttribute("debug","no") === "yes") {
    ctx.debug = true;
  }

  var obj = {};
  var parentNode = ctx.stack.length ? ctx.stack[ctx.stack.length - 1] : null;
  var capture = ctx.captureStack.length ? ctx.captureStack[ctx.captureStack.length - 1] : null;

  // Capture only if not already claimed
  if(capture && !capture.claimed) {
    if(!capture.hasValue) {
      capture.value = obj;
      capture.hasValue = true;
      capture.claimed = true; // <-- consume capture for direct child only
    } else if(ctx.debug) {
      ctx.warnings.push("json-obj: capture collision (extra nested value ignored)");
    }
  } else if(Array.isArray(parentNode)) {
    parentNode.push(obj);
  } else if(ctx.root === null) {
    ctx.root = obj;
  } else if(ctx.debug) {
    ctx.warnings.push("json-obj: unattached object (no array parent, root already set)");
  }

  ctx.stack.push(obj);
  this.didPushToStack = true;
  this.jsonBuilderContext = ctx;
};

JsonObjWidget.prototype.refresh = function(changedTiddlers) {
  var changedAttributes = this.computeAttributes();
  if(Object.keys(changedAttributes).length > 0) {
    this.refreshSelf();
    return true;
  }
  return this.refreshChildren(changedTiddlers);
};

exports["json-obj"] = JsonObjWidget;

})();
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"widget","created":"20260221095624306","modified":"20260305110625330","tags":""},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-prop.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-prop.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/yourname/json-dsl/json-prop.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: widget
\*/
(function(){

/*jslint node: true, browser: true */
/*global $tw: false */
"use strict";

var Widget = require("$:/core/modules/widgets/widget.js").widget;

function getJsonContext(widget) {
  var w = widget;
  while(w) {
    if(w.jsonBuilderContext) {
      return w.jsonBuilderContext;
    }
    w = w.parentWidget;
  }
  return null;
}

function JsonPropWidget(parseTreeNode,options) {
  this.initialise(parseTreeNode,options);
}
JsonPropWidget.prototype = new Widget();

JsonPropWidget.prototype.render = function(parent,nextSibling) {
  this.parentDomNode = parent;
  this.execute();

  if(this.useBodyValue && this.currentObject && this.key) {
    this.renderChildren(parent,nextSibling);

    var ctx = this.jsonBuilderContext;
    if(ctx && ctx.captureStack && ctx.captureStack.length) {
      var cap = ctx.captureStack[ctx.captureStack.length - 1];
      if(cap && cap.hasValue) {
        this.currentObject[this.key] = cap.value;
      } else if(!this.omitEmpty) {
        this.currentObject[this.key] = null;
      }
      ctx.captureStack.pop();
    }
  }
};

JsonPropWidget.prototype.execute = function() {
  this.computeAttributes();
  this.makeChildWidgets();

  this.key = this.getAttribute("key","");
  this.type = (this.getAttribute("type","string") || "string").toLowerCase();
  this.omitEmpty = this.getAttribute("omit-empty","no") === "yes";
  this.filter = this.getAttribute("filter",null);

  if(!this.key) return;

  var ctx = getJsonContext(this);
  if(!ctx || !ctx.stack || !ctx.stack.length) return;

  var current = ctx.stack[ctx.stack.length - 1];
  if(!current || Array.isArray(current)) return;

  this.jsonBuilderContext = ctx;
  this.currentObject = current;

  this.useBodyValue = !!(this.parseTreeNode.children && this.parseTreeNode.children.length);
  if(this.useBodyValue) {
    ctx.captureStack.push({
      hasValue: false,
      claimed: false,   // <-- important
      value: null,
      key: this.key
    });
    return;
  }

  var value;
  if(this.filter) {
    var results = this.wiki.filterTiddlers(this.filter,this);
    value = results.length ? results[0] : "";
  } else {
    value = this.getAttribute("value","");
  }

  if(this.omitEmpty && (value === "" || value == null)) return;

  current[this.key] = this.coerceValue(value,this.type);
};

JsonPropWidget.prototype.coerceValue = function(value,type) {
  switch(type) {
    case "string": return String(value);
    case "number": return (value === "" ? null : Number(value));
    case "boolean":
    case "bool": return /^(true|yes|1)$/i.test(String(value));
    case "null": return null;
    case "raw":
      try { return JSON.parse(value); } catch (e) { return value; }
    default: return String(value);
  }
};

JsonPropWidget.prototype.refresh = function(changedTiddlers) {
  return this.refreshChildren(changedTiddlers) || (this.refreshSelf(), true);
};

exports["json-prop"] = JsonPropWidget;

})();
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"widget","created":"20260221095715923","modified":"20260305110625330","tags":""},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-save.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-save.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-save.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: widget

Writes built JSON into a tiddler field
\*/
(function(){

"use strict";

var Widget = require("$:/core/modules/widgets/widget.js").widget;

function getJsonContext(widget) {
	var w = widget;
	while(w) {
		if(w.jsonBuilderContext) {
			return w.jsonBuilderContext;
		}
		w = w.parentWidget;
	}
	return null;
}

function JsonSaveWidget(parseTreeNode,options) {
	this.initialise(parseTreeNode,options);
}
JsonSaveWidget.prototype = new Widget();

JsonSaveWidget.prototype.render = function(parent,nextSibling) {
	this.parentDomNode = parent;
	this.execute();
};

JsonSaveWidget.prototype.execute = function() {
	this.computeAttributes();
	this.saveTiddler = this.getAttribute("$tiddler","");
	this.saveField = this.getAttribute("$field","text");
	this.saveIndent = parseInt(this.getAttribute("indent","2"),10);
	if(isNaN(this.saveIndent)) this.saveIndent = 2;

	if(!this.saveTiddler) return;

	var ctx = getJsonContext(this);
	if(!ctx) return;

	var json;
	try {
		json = JSON.stringify(ctx.root, null, this.saveIndent);
	} catch(e) {
		json = '{"error":"JSON save: stringify failed"}';
	}

	this.wiki.setText(this.saveTiddler, this.saveField, undefined, json);
};

JsonSaveWidget.prototype.refresh = function(changedTiddlers) {
	this.refreshSelf();
	return true;
};

exports["json-save"] = JsonSaveWidget;

})();
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"widget","created":"20260303120000000","modified":"20260305110625330","tags":""},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-val.js":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/json-val.js","text":"/*\
title: $:/plugins/yourname/json-dsl/json-val.js
type: application/javascript
module-type: widget
\*/
(function(){
"use strict";
var Widget = require("$:/core/modules/widgets/widget.js").widget;

function getJsonContext(widget){ var w=widget; while(w){ if(w.jsonBuilderContext){return w.jsonBuilderContext;} w=w.parentWidget; } return null; }

function JsonValWidget(parseTreeNode,options){ this.initialise(parseTreeNode,options); }
JsonValWidget.prototype = new Widget();

JsonValWidget.prototype.render = function(parent,nextSibling){ this.parentDomNode = parent; this.execute(); };

JsonValWidget.prototype.execute = function() {
  this.computeAttributes();
  var ctx = getJsonContext(this), cap, value, type;
  if(!ctx || !ctx.captureStack || !ctx.captureStack.length) return;

  cap = ctx.captureStack[ctx.captureStack.length - 1];
  if(!cap) return;

  // Only direct child of json-prop may claim capture
  if(cap.claimed) {
    if(ctx.debug) { ctx.warnings.push("json-val: capture collision (extra scalar ignored)"); }
    return;
  }

  value = this.getAttribute("value","");
  type = (this.getAttribute("type","string") || "string").toLowerCase();

  cap.value = this.coerceValue(value,type);
  cap.hasValue = true;
  cap.claimed = true; // <-- consume capture
};

JsonValWidget.prototype.coerceValue = function(value,type){
  switch(type){
    case "string": return String(value);
    case "number": return (value === "" ? null : Number(value));
    case "boolean":
    case "bool": return /^(true|yes|1)$/i.test(String(value));
    case "null": return null;
    case "raw": try { return JSON.parse(value); } catch(e) { return value; }
    default: return String(value);
  }
};

JsonValWidget.prototype.refresh = function(changedTiddlers){ this.refreshSelf(); return true; };

exports["json-val"] = JsonValWidget;
})();
","type":"application/javascript","module-type":"widget","created":"20260221095826387","modified":"20260305110625330","tags":""},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/readme":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/readme","created":"20260305110625331","modified":"20260305110625331","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"! json dsl

Declarative JSON builder widgets for TiddlyWiki. Compose JSON structures using widget syntax instead of string manipulation.

!! Key features

* ''Composable widget set'' — `<$json-obj>`, `<$json-array>`, `<$json-prop>`, `<$json-val>`, `<$json-item>`
* ''Output widgets'' — emit to tiddler field (`<$json-save>`) or action variable (`<$json-emit>`)
* ''Debug widget'' — `<$json-debug>` for inspecting output during development
* ''Widget tree integration'' — seamless integration with TiddlyWiki's widget tree and filter expressions

!! Prerequisites

No external prerequisites.

!! Quick start

Build JSON declaratively: `<$json-obj><$json-prop name="key"><$json-val value="hello"/></$json-prop></$json-obj>`. Use `<$json-emit>` to output the result.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/settings":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/json-dsl/settings","created":"20260305110625331","modified":"20260305110625331","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<div class="rr-settings-info-card">
No configurable settings. Future settings will appear here.
</div>
"}}}
